Yulang `v0.1.0-alpha.3` is an alpha release focused on the new directed
effect-inference core and handler-hygiene soundness.

## Highlights

- Reworked effect subtraction around directed stack weights, keeping left
  active-push obligations and right pure-pop obligations separate.
- Updated row splitting so handlers consume only
  `target head ∩ Common(active left stack)`, preserving principal residual
  effects instead of over-consuming rows.
- Erased left-side filters before storing solver bounds, while keeping the
  required static family checks.
- Added invariant family-argument constraints when effect-family paths meet in
  row split, residual subtraction, duplicate row heads, filter checks, and
  common-stack checks.
- Tightened cleanup liveness so only active covariant pushes keep stack ids
  alive.
- Fixed runtime marker hygiene by painting escaping request payloads and by
  using scope-entry except snapshots for guard coloring. This preserves both
  nested-handler directions: outer guards still mark requests hidden by later
  inner scopes, while inner guards do not repaint requests already excepted at
  entry.
- Updated the hosted playground/docs bundle and smoke-tested the main effect
  examples.

## Install

Unix-like systems:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://yulang.momota.pw/install.sh | sh -s -- --version v0.1.0-alpha.3
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Invoke-WebRequest https://yulang.momota.pw/install.ps1 -OutFile install.ps1
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\install.ps1 -Version v0.1.0-alpha.3
```

## Known Limitations

- The language and standard library remain alpha-stage and may still change.
- The exact worklist termination story for directed stack weights is documented
  separately from principal type soundness and still needs more implementation
  diagnostics.
- LSP and diagnostics are usable but still need clearer type-origin
  explanations.
- Runtime performance for continuation-heavy programs is still an active
  optimization area.
